tool.name: EnzyWizard Dock
tool.description: Perform molecular docking of one or multiple substrates with a cleaned protein structure
tool.command: exec 3>&1 4>&2; exec > enzywizard.log 2>&1; #set $input_name = $input_path.element_identifier ln -s '$input_path' '$input_name' && mkdir -p substrate_dir && unzip -q '$substrate_zip' -d substrate_dir && enzywizard-dock -i '$input_name' -s '$substrate_names' -d substrate_dir -o ./dock_output --max_docking_attempt_num '$advanced.max_docking_attempt_num' $advanced.no_early_stop --exhaustiveness '$advanced.exhaustiveness' --cpu "\${GALAXY_SLOTS:-1}" --min_rad '$advanced.min_rad' --max_rad '$advanced.max_rad' --min_volume '$advanced.min_volume' #if $advanced.catalytic_residue: --catalytic_residue '$advanced.catalytic_residue' #end if #if $advanced.catalytic_site_coord: --catalytic_site_coord '$advanced.catalytic_site_coord' #end if #if $advanced.box_size: --box_size '$advanced.box_size' #end if && cat enzywizard.log >&4

expand.name: substrate_zip
expand.label: Substrate directory (zip)
expand.help: Zip archive containing substrate SDF files.

tool.help: Perform molecular docking of one or multiple substrates with a cleaned protein structure. Directory inputs are represented as zip datasets and unpacked explicitly inside this wrapper command in the Galaxy job working directory. Outputs are exposed as discovered dataset collections.
